programme.desc: Tinker lectures Lovejoy on the importance of not abusing his ability to gather ill-gotten gains. However, the antiques dealer briefly turns his back on his own ethical standards, only to soon regret his transgression. Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton star.(n)

programme.desc: Despatched by Munro to kill a French colonel, Sharpe is wounded in battle and hovers at death's door - but Harper and a mute nun nurse him back to health. Once recovered, he sets out in search of vengeance with a newly forged sword. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean and Daragh O'Malley.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads find themselves in a tight spot after a job turns to disaster in Russia. Penniless and desperate to go home, they visit the British Embassy, where news filters through of a department that allows men to travel the world fixing up British Government property. Oz's criminal past comes back to haunt them and threatens to ruin their chances, but, thankfully, Neville has friends in high places who save the day.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads prepare to start work on the Ambassador's residence in Havana, but get off to a bad start with their new gaffer, who makes it clear he considers them a bunch of cowboys. Wyman catches the eye of a sultry local beauty during a night out, while Oz is captivated by the charms of the Cuban National Ballet's leading lady - and turns to Barry for advice on romance. Tim Healy, Noel Clark, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo help reorganise the seed museum owned by the descendants of famous botanist Edwin Pargeter - but it is not long before a man is found dead in the family's garden. In an effort to trace his killer, Rosemary involves colleagues from the Royal Horticultural Society at Kew Gardens, and soon the race is on to identify a batch of mysterious seeds before it falls into the wrong hands. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are invited to help create an Arabian-style garden in Malaga. Before long, a local resident is found dead, and a mysterious landowner is the chief suspect. When it transpires that someone has blocked the mountainside channels on which the new garden will rely for its supply of water, it appears the precious commodity may provide a motive for the killing. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to re-create a Victorian garden in Regent's Park, where they meet all manner of intriguing characters - but the oasis of calm in central London is shattered by the discovery of a body. The victim is a respected Harley Street psychiatrist - whose three-legged dog appears to hold the key to solving the case. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A bride-to-be and professional tennis player is found dead after her hen party, and the chief bridesmaid, best man and groom-to-be all come under the team's suspicion.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates the murder of a B-movie director when the production of Demon Brides comes to Kembleford. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: The discovery of a concrete-encased body at a Docklands building site leads to conflict between DCI Hunt and the location's owner. Investigations are further hampered by the disappearance of crucial evidence, an incident that points to a mole in the detective's own team. Meanwhile, Alex discovers that her fascination with the case is distracting her from returning home.(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene learns crooked cops are going to interrupt a bullion robbery and steal the gold for themselves - a discovery that gives him the chance to root out corruption in the force once and for all. Meanwhile, the arrival of an attractive woman highlights the cracks in his relationship with Alex, who is making him doubt her trustworthiness.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of a two-part story by Lynda La Plante in which the team investigate the death of a prostitute who plunged from the balcony of a block of luxury apartments. They discover she was the daughter of a famous Seventies TV star murdered 12 years previously, whose killer was never traced, and try to find a link between the two crimes. David Hayman stars as the newly promoted DCS Michael Walker, alongside Victoria Smurfit, Colin Salmon and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The normally resourceful barrister struggles to come up with a defence when confronted with a client who openly admits to the court that she killed her husband. To make matters worse, he is also co-opted into acting on behalf of wayward colleague Jeremy Aldermarten, whose tendency to take liberties has led to accusations of professional misconduct. Drama, starring John Thaw and Lynda Steadman.(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A victimised German pensioner in a residential home asks Hetty to help trace his long-lost sister. Intent on setting the world to rights, the tenacious sleuth soon finds the elderly gentleman has a few surprises in store. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge, Richard Pasco and Suzanne Maddock.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Reg organises a half-centenary celebration of Sun Hill, attended by dignitaries including former DCS Charles Barnett. On a less happy note, Amanda Prosser's insensitive treatment of a bereaved father provokes a hostage crisis, and the real Gabriel Kent turns up out of the blue, spelling big trouble for the unscrupulous copper who has assumed his identity.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. In an episode originally broadcast live, distraught dad Jeff Clarke holds the Sun Hill stalwarts at gunpoint, prompting a desperate response from Dan Casper - who ends up getting shot, along with the real Gabriel Kent. Fortunately, Terry Perkins is able to stay cool and assume the role of mediator, helping to defuse the situation while Acting Superintendent Amanda Prosser flounders helplessly.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor and Nurse Carr carry out tuberculosis tests in the East End slums, while Robert's electro-therapy treatment causes problems. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter _ but when his housekeeper dies suddenly, she changes her opinion. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby.(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret.(n)

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia expresses her reservations about the artist Lloyd Ellis Parker to James, while Rene is distraught to learn about Sarah's pregnancy. Meanwhile, Carolyn confronts George about Regina, and Elizabeth has lunch with Douglas Goddard.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.sub-title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: George takes Sarah to hospital after finding her at the side of the road, while Olivia continues her relationship with Lloyd, but is distraught when James discovers the affair.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Rose's love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddy's bedroom claims to be his fiance. Starring Patricia Routledge.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toy boy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends the christening of Daisy's granddaughter, and is displeased to learn the child's parents are not married. With some encouragement from Richard, she agrees to keep an open mind, but is later irked when the mother and child go missing. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the woman's younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthia's depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith.(n)

programme.title: Harrow
programme.desc: The investigator tackles a case in North Queensland involving the discovery on a beach of the body of a parks and conservation officer with a number of shark bites on it. Poisonous jellyfish found in the victim's stomach raise suspicions as to the true nature of his demise. Harrow reconnects with Dr Laurie Badcoe, his other mentor besides Jack, who assures him that Francis Chester is dead. Ioan Gruffudd stars.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: When a troubled gambler is murdered at a greyhound track it seems that his enormous debts have cost him his life. The investigation leads Ross and the team from race-fixing to sex tapes, and in the midst of it all one of their suspects - who include the victim's ex-wife and a dog handler with dark secrets - turns up dead. Detective drama, starring John Michie, Blythe Duff and Alex Norton.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: A woman leaves her estate to her son Alexander, who disappeared in Italy during World War Two on condition he claims the inheritance by 2010. When a mysterious stranger turns up at the funeral to claim the inheritance, he is called an imposter by the rest of the family. The quest for the truth finds Dalziel and Pascoe uncovering dark family secrets, including murder, betrayal, mad dogs, gay bars, young love and a revealing birthmark. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Margaret Tyzack, Jamie Glover and Jack Hedley.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth, Joe Swift and the team end a week of programmes looking back at the best of the horticultural event by focusing firmly on the public's favourite gardens, plants and personalities. There is a look at how the Queen and members of the royal family have been involved over the years and Sophie and Joe reveal the designer who has won the BBC/RHS People's Choice Garden of the Decade.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: The actor and film-maker celebrates the efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers, joining them as they help with everything from shopping to delivering hospital equipment.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents a selection of ideas for making the most of a budget, looking back at inexpensive landscaping techniques from past medal-winning gardens. Plus, packing borders or containers without breaking the bank and making displays from cheap bunches of flowers.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in Essex that produces 400,000 cereal bars a day, and follows the production process from the arrival of the ingredients to the eventual dispatch, discovering the importance of getting the perfect balance between honey and glucose. Cherry Healey travels to South Africa to help with the macadamia harvest, where a quarter of the world's supply of these nuts are farmed. Historian Ruth Goodman climbs a mountain in the Lake District to investigate the history of one of the oldest forms of snack bar - the Kendal Mint Cake.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Puerto Rico: Island of Enchantment - Natural World
programme.desc: After being colonised by humans, Puerto Rico's rich natural resources were rapidly exploited for agriculture, industry and urban development. This documentary follows a conservation team fighting to save the most endangered creatures from extinction after they have been pushed to the edge of existence by human encroachment. David Attenborough narrates.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts a game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: A Tale of Five Temples: The Story of Hinduism in Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of five Hindu temples across Britain, tracing their stories and the spread of Hinduism in the UK. Featuring the elaborate rituals and the moving stories of Hindu worshippers, the film gives an insight into the sheer variety of Hindu beliefs across Britain. Narrated by Naga Munchetty.(n)

programme.title: Astaire and Rogers Sing the Great American Songbook
programme.desc: A compilation of hits performed by Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers, all written by renowned American songwriters including George and Ira Gershwin, Irving Berlin, Jerome Kern and Dorothy Fields, Harold Arlen and Johnny Mercer.(n)

programme.title: Carefree (1938)
programme.desc: A woman has doubts about her impending wedding and pays a visit to her fiance's therapist friend. However, the counselling does not go to plan and she develops romantic feelings for the doctor, forcing him to resort to hypnotism to cure her infatuation - with unforeseen consequences. Musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers and Ralph Bellamy.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by antiques experts James Lewis and Caroline Hawley at Wolverhampton Art Gallery, which boasts an extensive collection of paintings. Turning up out of the hundreds of bags and boxes is a sword with a dark history and an early Moorcroft vase. Host Paul also explores some of the region's industrial past.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Chizzy Akudolu, author and comedian Charlie Higson, historian Kate Williams and comedian Tom Allen take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, culminating in the week's winner being crowned.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Longleat keeper Caleb wants to find out who are the heroes and who are the zeros in lion country with a test of their intelligence. Calling on departments from across the estate, he tries to create a contraption that will see the lions work together as a team to release their food. Meanehile, Ben Fogle tries to mend his ways with Rio the tearaway cockatoo and Longleat's very own spiderman tries to save a rare arachnid.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef's culinary travels take him through Mexico City, and Puebla, where he samples Mexico's most iconic dish, the meat stew mole. Later, in the deserts of Oaxaca, he discovers how one of the country's most famous drinks, mezcal is made.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don, Joe Swift and the team look at the legends and legacies that have made the Chelsea Flower Show the greatest event in the horticultural calendar. They discover what it takes to become an icon at this show, talk to the Chelsea designers and plants men and women that have broken records, won global recognition and inspired generations of gardeners. Plus, how movers and shakers from other creative fields have been influenced by their visits to the event over the years.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With lockdown affecting new filming, Monty Don is at Longmeadow to take an indulgent, horticultural romp through the Gardeners' World archives. From delphiniums to clematis, irises, orchids and dahlias expert growers share their passion along with hints and tips. Plus, Carol Klein visits a garden in Worcestershire where she discovers picture perfect peonies, Adam Frost travels to the Cotswolds and finds out what happens when plants are brought together with imagination and daring and Nick Bailey offers a guide to the world of Britain's favourite flower - the rose.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The host reinvents his music show to accommodate the requirements of the lockdown, joined - virtually - in this edition by grime pioneer Dizzee Rascal. The east London rapper talks through his musical journey and some of the artists that were part of it, illustrated by clips from the BBC archives. His choices reflect the diverse array of genres from indie to grime and jazz, that have influenced his music, alongside memories from a career that kicked off with his Mercury Prize-winning debut Boy in Da Corner in 2003. Plus, as part of the show's focus on new music and artists, a unique performance by a contemporary artist.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Spotlight (2015)
programme.desc: A team of investigative reporters for a Boston newspaper investigate reports of children being molested within the Catholic church. The journalists discover the situation is far worse than it seemed, as they find a conspiracy among the city's legal, religious and government establishments to cover up decades of abuse. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Mark Ruffalo, Michael Keaton, Rachel McAdams and Stanley Tucci.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo returns to explore more of the US rail network, embarking on the first leg of a journey across Alaska. He begins his journey amid the snow-capped mountains of the Kenal Peninsula, where he discovers that 150 years ago, Alaska was a Russian colony. Moving on, in Seward he feeds a rescued sea otter with a fearsome bite and in Anchorage he joins the US Arctic Warriors for parachute training.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Three best friends plan an inflatables-themed wedding.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood share some eerie events from the natural world, including a dust storm which turned an entire city red, and a cloud shaped like a UFO.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and the team try their hand at fig pie rolling in Cheshire.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall reveals the advanced camera technology and tricks of the trade that allow the Deadly 60 team to catch dramatic wildlife shots, including the breathtaking breach attack of a great white shark.(n)

programme.title: Six Robots & Us
programme.desc: A unique experiment exploring the latest innovations in robotics, to examine how useful robots can be in enhancing the lives of ordinary families. In this episode, experts from four universities deliver four robots - Fitbot, Carebot, Tutorbot and Beam - to families around the country, with Psychologist Dr Caroline Jay and robotics specialist, Professor Jonathan Rossiter watching their every move.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age Giants
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explains what the remains of mammals reveal about how they fared in cold climates. In Transylvanian caves, she discovers evidence of a conflict between a cave bear and a lion, both of which became victims of a deep freeze that swept across Europe, while in Siberia she finds that the well-preserved remains of a female woolly rhinoceros demonstrate it was perfectly evolved to face the bitter conditions in Yakutsk - the coldest city on Earth. Alice also travels to a Jersey cave, where archaeological findings shed light on how Ice Age human beings and Neanderthals fashioned stone tools to hunt mammoths.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Fifty years ago, 34 per cent of the British population worked in manufacturing. Factories mostly employed women, who made an array of clothes and household items that were sold all over the world. Alex Jones presents this documentary in which a group of modern workers go back in time to learn what life was like for women through the 1960s to the 1980s. They start in a re-creation of 1968, when the bulk of high street clothes were made in the UK. The reality of the production line is a rude awakening for many - long monotonous hours with short breaks and few distractions - a situation made even worse when they open their pay packets.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: The Great British Bake Off 2015 champion Nadiya Hussain embarks on a culinary road trip around Britain, meeting with producers, farmers and fishermen across the country as she creates her own recipes inspired by their produce. In the first episode Nadiya begins her tour in the Home Counties, where she visits an asparagus farm in Oxford, before trying her hand at food smoking in Milton Keynes.(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of sugar-rich foods in Britain, and try to make their own versions of their favourite childhood sweets. The duo also prepare a cheesecake and a sweet-and-sour dish.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk presents as Anna Haugh prepares a dish served at a royal wedding and Polly Russell goes to Gloucester to trace the history of an ancient royal delicacy.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of TV interviews with American actor, director, writer and producer Orson Welles, whose significant contribution to cinema included Citizen Kane and The Third Man. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Citizen Kane (1941)
programme.desc: After the death of an influential newspaper magnate, an inquisitive reporter begins piecing together the details of his life, discovering an epic tale of ambition and conflict - all the while trying to discover the meaning behind the millionaire's enigmatic last word. Oscar-winning drama, written, directed by and starring Orson Welles. With Joseph Cotten, Dorothy Comingore and William Alland.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall visits the Wye Valley, hoping to encounter the barbel, which has fascinated him since his childhood. With the help of a local angling guide, he sets out to locate the 'prince of the river'. At the cliffs around Chepstow, he finds out how keen bird-watchers are helping to protect the nests of rare peregrine falcons, and in the north of the valley, Hugh assists in reintroducing tiny harvest mice to a wildlife-friendly farm.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire as hundreds of visitors arrive with their family heirlooms and the experts pick out the best items to go off to auction. Paul also investigates the history of nearby Quarrybank Mill.(n)

programme.title: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner?
programme.desc: Anne Robinson hosts a programme in which celebrities reveal their ideal dinner parties, from the location to the food and guests. In this edition, actress Joan Collins chooses guests including a former American president and an acclaimed British playwright, and reveals her admiration for an ancient femme fatale.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at TV appearances made by the Dynasty star, with the archive footage telling the story of her life and career.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile rail tour of south-east Asia reaches Malaysia. On the island of Penang, he traces the origins of the former British colony in Georgetown, rides one of the world's steepest funicular railways and is enchanted by exotic specimens at Malaysia's first butterfly sanctuary. Travelling on the nation's hi-tech railway network Michael visits its wettest town, Taiping, and learns how the discovery of tin made this land a prized possession for Britain. Next stop is the regal town of Kuala Kangsar, home to the Sultan of Perak and to one of Malaysia's most prestigious schools.(n)

programme.title: Citizens of Boomtown: The Story of the Boomtown Rats
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the Irish band led by Bob Geldof, who scored hits in the late 1970s and early 80s with the likes of Rat Trap and I Don't Like Mondays, before disbanding a year after Geldof's hugely successful Live Aid event in 1985. The group reformed in 2013 and released their seventh studio album in March 2020.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.sub-title: The Story of Top of the Pops 1978
programme.desc: Documentary about the change of musical direction in 1978, when pop singles were slowly ousted by disco, punk and new wave artists. The programme features contributions by members of the Boomtown Rats, Squeeze, Boney M and Sham 69 - among others - and archive performances by the likes of Elvis Costello, the Undertones, Ian Dury and the Blockheads and the Jam.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1978
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pops 1978: Big Hits
programme.desc: A selection of classic songs from 1978, a year in which punk music and new wave rebellion co-existed with pop, disco, soul, reggae and prog rock. Featuring appearances on the show by Ian Dury, Elvis Costello, Blondie, Bob Marley, the Boomtown Rats, Kate Bush, Sham 69, Brotherhood of Man, Dan Hill, Boney M, 10cc and Manfred Mann's Earth Band.(n)

programme.title: The Age of Shadows (2016)
programme.desc: In Japanese-occupied Seoul in the 1920s, the police try to infiltrate the Korean resistance as they plan an attack. Period thriller, starring Yoo Gong and Kang-ho Song. In Korean and Japanese with English subtitles.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Basingstoke, Hampshire, to breathe new life into mother-of-five Tania's disorganised garden and turn it into a child-safe and enchanting space.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter K. Highlights include Rick Stein cooking lamb koftas and Raymond Blanc serving up kougelhopf.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is on her home turf in Gloucestershire, where she helps with important conservation work at Slimbridge Wetland Centre, gets a ringside seat to watch some kingfisher parents busy feeding their young and explores the treasures of an ancient bluebell wood. Adam Henson reveals how his horses are helping to create a wildlife haven and Tom Heap investigates how fly-tipping is blighting the countryside more than ever.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With lockdown affecting new filming, Monty Don is at Longmeadow to take an indulgent, horticultural romp through the Gardeners' World archives. From delphiniums to clematis, irises, orchids and dahlias expert growers share their passion along with hints and tips. Plus, Carol Klein visits a garden in Shropshire where she discovers picture perfect plant combinations, Adam Frost travels to the Cotswolds and finds out what happens when plants are brought together with imagination and daring and Nick Bailey offers a guide to the world of Britain's favourite flower - the rose.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith explores the myth and legend surrounding some common garden birds, while Anne Lundon meets the man who fought for his remote village to be connected by road. Arlene Stuart finds out about the incredible lifecycle of the eel and Dougie Vipond learns how rural tourist businesses hope to fight back after lockdown. Plus, a day in the life of a country postie during the pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson hankers after hostas, Kirsty Wilson harks back to the '70s to create an easy terrarium and Chris Beardshaw deals with a very bad bout of Box Blight.(n)

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prove that home-made can beat shop-bought when they prepare gravlax, jam doughnuts and their own corned beef.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: The Unnatural History of London: Natural World
programme.desc: Exploring the London's surprising and secret wildlife, including seals, parakeets, badgers, foxes and even pelicans who have made London their home. The programme also focuses on the admirers of these animals, from Billingsgate fish porters and Indian chefs to Crayfish Bob, who scours the canals for foreign species.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by the Oscar-winning actor John Wayne, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career that led to him becoming considered a US national icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin investigates the history of highwaymen in Surrey in an episode from the Watts Gallery, where visitors arrive with their antiques and collectables to get them valued.(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers.(n)

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary exploring the country in the aftermath of the recent humanitarian crisis in which thousands of Rohingya Muslims were driven from their homes by the military. Simon visits Burma's Buddhist heartlands, including the ancient capital of Bagan, and meets the city's controversial nationalist monks. While the region affected by the military crackdown is off limits, he is able to visit the world's biggest refugee camp in Bangladesh, and meet people traumatised by the violence.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from the past 17 series of the business ideas contest, featuring Dragons past and present getting to grips with ingenious inventions and bizarre money-making schemes. The show recalls some of the harshest dismissals, but also the times when entrepreneurs got the chance to make their dream a reality.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: The rapper prepares to open a show for big-name star Meek Mill. He asks GaTa to be his hype man on stage. However, as GaTa's behaviour becomes more and more erratic, it may be impossible to keep his biggest secret from his new friends.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: The rapper and his crew travel to his home town of Philadelphia, where he is about to give his first big live show as opening act for Meek Mill.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: A murderous raid backfires for EZ and the gang when a covert ally is caught, while Angel's animosity to his brother troubles their chief.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ and Angel embark on a rift-healing road trip. Back home an even older relationship is revisited, and Emily loses her cool.(n)

programme.title: Dazzling Duets at the BBC
programme.desc: Memorable performances from the BBC archive, featuring singers and musicians from various genres on stage together in shows ranging from Parkinson to the Proms. Featuring appearances by Oscar Peterson, Kiri te Kanawa, Nigel Kennedy, Larry Adler, Bela Fleck and Ballake Sissoko.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on August 31, featuring performances by Damian, Debbie Gibson, Eurythmics, Guns N' Roses, Tears for Fears, Donna Summer and Big Fun.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: With cinemas closed and the nation on lockdown, Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo turn their attention to the small screen, presenting a guide to the best - and worst - of streaming culture, across movies and premium TV. Here, they take a critical look at The First Team and I Know This Much Is True.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first shown on September 7, featuring performances by Marillion, Richard Marx, Alyson Williams, Starlight, Prince, Depeche Mode and Jason Donovan.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Eighties
programme.desc: Showcasing a selection of musical performances from the BBC archives, including Kool and the Gang, the Pointer Sisters, Grace Jones, Cameo, Bobby Womack, Sade, Alexander O'Neal and Whitney Houston.(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri.(n)

programme.title: Sings Stevie Wonder
programme.desc: A trawl through the BBC archives, unearthing 50 years of covers of Wonder's classic songbook, featuring artists including Cilla Black, Jimmy Helms, Dionne Warwick, the Osmonds, India Arie and James Morrison. Among the clips is a storming performance of Ed Sheeran with Jools Holland and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra taking on Master Blaster (Jammin').(n)

programme.title: People's History of Pop
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne celebrates music from 1986-1996 that had the power to unite fans like never before, which started with a move away from the mainstream towards alternative music scenes. The episode looks back at the influential acid house wave that hit in 1988, the burgeoning UK hip-hop scene, and finally turns to the massive pop bands that exploded on the music scene, such as Take That and Spice Girls.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form focuses on the years 1933 to 1945, when the country music industry evolved with Nashville as its heart. The genre grew in popularity during the Great Depression and the Second World War, when America fell in love with singing cowboys, Texas Swing and the Grand Ole Opry's Roy Acuff. Contributors include Emmylou Harris, Vince Gill, Marty Stuart, Ray Benson, Merle Haggard, Willie Nelson and Ricky Skaggs. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri.(n)

programme.title: Dazzling Duets at the BBC
programme.desc: Memorable performances from the BBC archive, featuring singers and musicians from various genres on stage together in shows ranging from Parkinson to the Proms. Featuring appearances by Oscar Peterson, Kiri te Kanawa, Nigel Kennedy, Larry Adler, Bela Fleck and Ballake Sissoko.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: With more than half of the world's primates under threat of extinction, Chris Packham narrates a look at what is being done to protect primates, with scientists making ground-breaking discoveries about this remarkable animal family. Researcher Dr Cat Hobaiter has spent 13 years studying chimpanzees in the jungles of Uganda, while on Thailand's East coast, Dr Amanda Tan reveals how long-tailed macaques use tools to harvest shellfish.(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper explores the American Northwest, revealing how culture was established on the coast that has lasted for over 10,000 years. He travels to southeast Alaska, British Columbia and Washington State to see how a complex society developed without agriculture, and discloses the hidden significance in totem poles, canoes and intricate textiles.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.sub-title: Party at the Esso Motor Hotel
programme.desc: Mayor Rettedal uses unorthodox methods to get Stavanger elected as the oil capital of Norway, while Anna does something she regrets at a party at the Esso Motor Hotel, and Toril reaches out to Bengt Hamre. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: It is time for the parliament to vote on which city is to become the oil capital of Norway, and everyone in town is eager to hear the result. Anna makes a decision with consequences for everyone around her. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: The life of acting legend Michael Caine, star of movies such as The Ipcress File (being shown next), The Italian Job, and The Dark Knight. Using rare TV interviews and classic archive clips, this programme tells the story of one of Britain's most successful actors. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on August 31, featuring performances by Damian, Debbie Gibson, Eurythmics, Guns N' Roses, Tears for Fears, Donna Summer and Big Fun.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first shown on September 7, featuring performances by Marillion, Richard Marx, Alyson Williams, Starlight, Prince, Depeche Mode and Jason Donovan.(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper explores the American Northwest, revealing how culture was established on the coast that has lasted for over 10,000 years. He travels to southeast Alaska, British Columbia and Washington State to see how a complex society developed without agriculture, and discloses the hidden significance in totem poles, canoes and intricate textiles.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Five percussion soloists compete for a place in the semi-finals, performing works by John Psathas, Sergei Golovko, Georges Aperghis, Astor Piazzolla and Jago Thornton. Anna Lapwood is joined by former percussion winner Owen Gunnell to review the performances, recorded in the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Cairngorms: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's largest national park, a land of extremes in the heart of the Highlands, where a diverse range of wildlife including golden eagles, ptarmigan, capercaillie, red deer and crested tits can be found. The programme explores the area through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a mountain guide, an artist, a gamekeeper and a nature writer. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: How Quizzing Got Cool: TV's Brains of Britain
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell, Judith Keppel, Chris Tarrant, Mark Labbett and Nicholas Parsons help Ben Miller trace the origins of the pro-quizzers; quiz show veterans who have emerged from the crowd of quiz aficionados in recent years to achieve fame and fortune for their general knowledge prowess.(n)

programme.title: Revisor by Crystal Pite and Jonathon Young
programme.desc: A performance of the critically acclaimed dance-theatre production, created by award-winning choreographer Crystal Pite and playwright Jonathon Young, recorded during its run at Sadler's Wells in March. Revisor is based on the well-known play The Government Inspector by Nikolai Gogol, and Young and Pite revised the archetypal comic plot to serve as the basis for a production that blends contemporary theatre and dance.(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.sub-title: Russia - A Century of Suspicion: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Military historian Saul David draws on Timewatch documentaries and a wide range of BBC archive to examine how television has portrayed Russia through the years.(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.sub-title: Hamza,Truc and Anushka: Truc has a little sleep - at school
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of ordinary children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having a very busy day. In Jordan, seven-year-old Hamza is having trouble getting up for school. But will he get there in time? At school in Vietnam, Truc, who's seven, plays lots of games, has a tasty lunch and a little sleep with her friends. And after school in India, six-year-old Anushka learns how to draw patterns on the ground called Rangoli with her aunt. In each film we see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Documentary-maker Stacey Dooley is stuck in her flat like many of the UK. But armed with social media and a network of contacts, she reaches out to people across the country in search of those who are helping others. They include Wigan teacher-turned-scrub maker Charlotte, who works 14-hour days to provide protective equipment for health services; charity worker Freya, who chose to continue her work in Greece rather than fly home to be with her mother; and Glasgow man Jonathan, just one of the volunteers providing food for the city's homeless.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Britannia's Burning: Fire on the Bridge
programme.desc: Archive footage and eyewitness accounts are combined to tell the story of the blaze 50 years ago that ravaged the railway bridge over the Menai Straits connecting Anglesey and the mainland. programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith explores the myth and legend surrounding some common garden birds, while Anne Lundon meets the man who fought for his remote village to be connected by road. Arlene Stuart finds out about the incredible lifecycle of the eel and Dougie Vipond learns how rural tourist businesses hope to fight back after lockdown. Plus, a day in the life of a country postie during the pandemic.(n)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan celebrates her 40th birthday with Ben in a small guesthouse they stayed at 20 years ago. However, a pair of ageing swingers ensure not everything goes to plan. Back at home, the children are left under Nick's supervision, which consists of helping Janey bribe Michael to keep quiet about the weekend's activities.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee thinks the twins are too young to be taught about sex at school and that their teacher Miss Anstis is too stuck up to teach it. He resolves that it is his duty as a father to teach his children everything he knows about the facts of life, discovering only when it is too late that his knowledge is somewhat limited. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A sci-fi and fantasy edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with answers that no-one else could think of. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade makes a huge decision about her reaching out to her birth mother. When marine veteran Nate comes into the hospital claiming to have fallen from his wheelchair during a stunt, he quickly bonds with Jacob, who is thrilled to treat someone with such an exciting life. Meanwhile, Fenisha disobeys Jan's orders when the pair attend the scene of an accident at a wrestling match. Back at the hospital, Jan hands her an official warning, putting her future in jeopardy. Starring Gabriella Leon and Charles Venn.(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes goes on a diet after her family's comments about her weight, but soon realises how difficult it will be to lay off the fish and chips. Cathy's new partner seems more interested in her mother when he visits the Brown household, and Rory has a professional dilemma after he discovers illegal drugs at work.(n)

programme.desc: A journalist follows Lance Armstrong's rise to dominate the world of professional cycling, even following a successful battle with cancer. Fact-based drama, starring Ben Foster, Chris O'Dowd and Dustin Hoffman.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a man find a low-maintenance home in rural Suffolk with a wildlife-friendly garden on a budget of £490,000. Plus, a look at traditional boatbuilding skills.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades visits Bradford to look for hidden gems. Sharon O'Connor gives four office chairs a stylish new look, while Sarah Paterson brings a wooden storage bench up to date.(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.desc: Agnes goes on a diet after her family's comments about her weight, but soon realises how difficult it will be to lay off the fish and chips. Cathy's new partner seems more interested in her mother when he visits the Brown household, and Rory has a professional dilemma after he discovers illegal drugs at work.(n)

programme.title: Snow Patrol at Ward Park 3
programme.desc: Snow Patrol perform a homecoming gig at Ward Park in Bangor, Co Down, as the band go on a journey through their early classics to songs from their most recent album. Celebrating 25 years in music, the rockers will be treating the crowd to hits from their breakthrough release Final Straw and its follow-up Eyes Open, and last year's album Wildness.(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Ian Wright and Alan Shearer to select the most memorable showpiece matches of all time in the world's oldest domestic cup competition.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Angela Scanlon and John Simm join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack, hoping to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts.(n)

programme.title: Barra on the Bann
programme.desc: Weather presenter Barra Best travels along the Bann, meeting people who live, work and relax on Northern Ireland's longest river. He begins his 100-mile journey at the source of the Bann on Slieve Muck in the Mourne Mountains, before staying overnight at an eco farm, and learns about a unique project to reintroduce red kites to the region.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by Adil Ray for a special edition marking the end of Ramadan, with dishes and ideas from chefs Asma Khan and Saliha Mahmood Ahmed. There are clips from the BBC food archive, including Rick Stein and Nigel Slater showing Eid delicacies from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Kathy Jones leads a service for the Sunday after Ascension from Bangor Cathedral, Gwynedd. The preacher is the Rt Rev Andy John, Bishop of Bangor, and the hymns are sung by a Songs of Praise congregation recorded in March 2018.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt help two teams look for items at the National Botanic Garden of Wales, and Eric Knowles looks at how the natural world has inspired art and antiques.(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins looks back on stories of faith from islands around the UK coastline, beginning by reflecting on her visit to Caldey Island off Tenby in Pembrokeshire.(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a man find a low-maintenance home in rural Suffolk with a wildlife-friendly garden on a budget of £490,000. Plus, a look at traditional boatbuilding skills.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades visits Bradford to look for hidden gems. Sharon O'Connor gives four office chairs a stylish new look, while Sarah Paterson brings a wooden storage bench up to date.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth, Joe Swift and the team end a week of programmes looking back at the best of the horticultural event by focusing firmly on the public's favourite gardens, plants and personalities. There is a look at how the Queen and members of the royal family have been involved over the years and Sophie and Joe reveal the designer who has won the BBC/RHS People's Choice Garden of the Decade.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher heads Hampton Court, not far from his west London home, to see how the team there keep the gardens and grounds in prime condition, taking the reins of shire horses as they pull and harrow the ground for the summer ahead. Kate Humble reports from her Wye Valley home patch on how local food producers are rallying round during the crisis, and Adam Henson turns out a new Gloucester calf onto spring pasture.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Concorde Hangar at the Aerospace Bristol museum, where the most surprising item brought in is a very valuable statue of Buddha. Other finds being valued include an intricate model of a vintage car made by a prisoner of war, an Elizabethan ring, a collection of classic guitars, and of course, a great variety of Concorde memorabilia.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: As the investigation continues, Carolyn finds out information is being withheld by those close to her, while Eve discovers a lead that might bring her closer to Villanelle. Meanwhile, Konstantin's loyalties are split, and Villanelle has had enough and decides on a different path. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh.(n)

Later, they travel to a place renowned for its spiritual enlightenment, but a therapeutic conversation takes a decidedly different turn as the brothers finally get to have their say and talk to Dougie about how they felt when he left the family home when they were children.(n)

However, when their raucous neighbours' wild parties disrupt their lives, they decide to fight back, leading to a vicious cycle of one-upmanship. Comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Zac Efron and Rose Byrne.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends go on an extended drinking binge in a bid to win a tour of the local brewery - but their bad behaviour lands them in trouble with owner Pawtucket Pat, who bears more than a passing resemblance to Willy Wonka. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois runs for the position of school board president but refuses to overturn a decision to sack a teacher, prompting Peter to compete in the election and launch a smear campaign against her. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alex Borstein and Seth MacFarlane.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets on the wrong side of socially inept gangster Big Fat Paulie, putting Lois in the sights of a hitman. The Griffins visit the Mob boss on the day of his daughter's wedding in the hope that he will call off the killing. Animated comedy, with the guest voice of Michael Chiklis.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine discovers Stan's love of figure-skating and decides to take it up so they can perform as a pair. However, his competitive nature leads him to look for a more able partner. Meanwhile, Steve's classmate brings his mail-order bride to school.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.sub-title: Failure Is Not a Factory-Installed Option
programme.desc: A car salesman gets the better of Stan in a transaction, making him realise he has lost his negotiation and interrogation skills. Unable to question suspects properly, he is suspended from his job. Meanwhile, Steve tries to use his father's car to impress the popular girls at school.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland is shocked to discover that the object of his father's affections - Donna's Auntie Momma - is really a man, as he and his new family celebrate their first Thanksgiving together. Meanwhile, Roberta plans to spend the day with Federline.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland Jr and Roberta go on a school trip, leaving Cleveland and Donna excited by the prospect of having the house to themselves. However, Rallo does his best to keep the couple apart.(n)

programme.title: The Almost Impossible Gameshow
programme.desc: Action-packed game show, with commentary from Irish comedy duo the Rubberbandits. Ten contestants are given 50 lives to take on a series of tricky challenges. The aim of the game is to complete five of them without losing all of their lives. Challenges include Tiny Bike, Dizzy Hurdle and Groin Croissant.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the 14th series of nationwide talent search, which sees a wide variety of acts, from comedians to contortionists, singers to spoon players, compete for the chance to win £250,000 and a coveted slot at the Royal Variety Performance. Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams are once again on hand to judge this week's applicants, and each of them has the chance to put their favourite contestant straight through to the live semi-finals by awarding them the Golden Buzzer.(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Chris Hoy, Martine McCutcheon and Faye Tozer to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity.(n)

programme.desc: A selection of home videos sent in by members of the public and presented in a format to entertain people of all ages, narrated by Harry Hill.(n)

programme.title: Space Jam (1996)
programme.desc: Aliens arrive on Earth to abduct the Looney Tunes gang as a new attraction at their failing intergalactic theme park. To keep their freedom, Bugs Bunny and friends challenge the invaders to a high-stakes basketball game, calling on Michael Jordan to save the day and send the extraterrestrials back where they came from. Live action and animated comedy, starring Bill Murray and Danny DeVito, with the voices of Billy West and Bill Farmer.(n)

programme.desc: Brian gets a job with prestigious magazine The New Yorker, but it is not long before his new employer discovers he did not graduate from college, and promptly fires him. Filled with a burning desire to vindicate himself, he resolves to finish his education.(n)

programme.desc: Jim has his vasectomy. Norris enjoys making Derek feel uncomfortable during his review meeting. Josie packs her bags and tells Don she's moving out.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths throw a birthday party for Tommy. Don offers an olive branch to Nick. Des phones Steph up and invites her round.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Frank row about him cancelling her credit card. Ned insists Dave stays away from them as his actions could see them lose the farm. Seth is attacked by two poachers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth lies injured where the poachers left him. Mandy is determined to apply for Frank's housekeeper's job. Emma has a shock in store for Zoe.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Meanwhile, there are embarrassing consequences for Oscar when he investigates Wetherby's mysterious lunchtime disappearances. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers meet their new neighbours' dog, and George discusses politics with his local MP. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.sub-title: Two Foot Two, Eyes of Blue
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy go babysitting, but the baby will not stop crying, and the drinks cupboard is locked. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina is sceptical about Mandy's chances of getting the housekeeper's job. Zak is confident he can beat Arthur 'The Destroyer' Simpkins. An angry Zoe confronts Kim.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is proud of Jan for attacking Kim. Zak frightens Eric into giving him a share of the profits from the fight. Zoe tells Frank about the church blessing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Phyllis warns Des against getting involved with Steph again. Betty finds her house ransacked by burglars. Leo asks Raquel out for dinner.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine spends the night at Des's. Don tells Martin he intends to fight Gail to keep his home. Raquel is thrilled when Leo asks her out again. Steph turns up to see Des.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the village prepares for the annual flower show, Isobel finds herself in conflict with Violet, and Mary is introduced to a potential suitor, but it would seem she only has eyes for Matthew. Anna realises who is to blame when a valuable snuff box goes missing, and Mrs Patmore tearfully reveals her fears to Carson that her career may be coming to an end. Meanwhile, O'Brien engineers a way for Edith to take revenge on Mary. Drama, starring Maggie Smith and Robert Bathurst.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Aunt Joan reveals she has a rare bird nesting on her land but wants to keep it a secret to deter prospective egg thieves. Sure enough, her tactless nephew opens his mouth to the local baker, who spies an opportunity to save his ailing business by making it a new attraction. Before long the peaceful community of Portwenn is over-run by twitchers eager to catch a sight of the bird, and Dr Ellingham finds himself in the locals' bad books yet again. Martin Clunes stars in this Christmas special of the comedy drama, originally shown in 2006.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: In Moorland, Somerset, Alan Titchmarsh surprises the Colquhouns, a couple devastated by the floods of 2014, which arrived in the wake of a major health problem. Sheila had to undergo treatment for breast cancer, but after moving back home, in the year the couple will retire and celebrate their 40th wedding anniversary, Alan and the team attempt to transform their flood-wrecked garden.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head for rural Cornwall, where they create a spectacular contemporary garden for Chris Finney, a former serviceman who was awarded the George Cross in 2003. A space age-inspired area - complete with solar dome - will test Chris's knowledge as a novice gardener, and provide a stimulating haven for his young family.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Jungle (1970)
programme.desc: A Victorian explorer recalls how he was recruited by an aristocrat to find her missing son in the African jungle. Teaming up with a fearless hunter, he hoped to find a rare bird on his journey - but instead fell into the clutches of an all-female tribe. Comedy, starring Sid James, Frankie Howerd, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family).(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.sub-title: ITV3 Most Wanted - A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The 'Most Wanted' episode of A Touch of Frost, starring David Jason as the titular detective, based on the character created in the novels of RD Wingfield. The series ran from 1992 to 2010, and viewers can take their pick from the 42 episodes filmed.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A depressive gambler is found dead, the apparent victim of suicide. Dr Jane Moore, who once treated the man, is adamant he wouldn't have taken his own life - a theory backed up by the post-mortem. Thrilled to be working with Jane again, Barnaby investigates the case - and it isn't long before another of the doctor's patients is killed. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Isla Blair, Jan Ravens and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gerald Hadleigh, the secretary of the local writers' circle in the tranquil village of Midsomer Worthy, is found battered to death on the eve of a visit by a best-selling novelist. Barnaby and Troy discover Hadleigh was a mystery man, with no relatives or marriage certificate to match the wedding photo on display in his house. To make matters worse, another body is then found. Mystery, guest starring Anna Massey and Una Stubbs.(n)

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan.(n)

programme.title: Great SAS Missions
programme.desc: Using interviews, computer graphics and reconstructions to recount the Second World War SAS mission to infiltrate enemy borders.
