programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: British people may be famous for a stiff upper lip, but divorcees Shirlie and Dave get up close and personal. It's clear these fiftysomethings are feeling frisky. Richard and Freya bond over a shared love of musical theatre, and 33-year-old mother-of-two and yoga teacher Abi says she is looking for no less than James Bond. Will nice guy Mark leave her shaken or stirred?(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Transsexual Charlie hopes to use his Irish charm to win the affections of 22-year-old Charlotte, who claims to have no gender boundaries. How will she feel when he tells her his secret? Retired market trader Billy (82) has his work cut out trying to tame divorced aromatherapist Sandra, and 20-year-old Rhys struggles to get a word in when he sits down for a date with chatty nanny Georgia.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Mother's boy Tiago plans to put his self-conscious bride Karlette on the big screen at his 1950s drive-in wedding. However, his attention-shunning fiancee wants her wedding day to be something intimate and contemporary.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In this revisit episode, chef Gordon Ramsay goes back to Mojito, the Junction (formerly known as Flamangos) and Bazzini to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use. Will he be pleased with their progress?(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Tom plans a wedding at his favourite theme park, but his down-to-earth fiancee Becca has always dreamed of being treated like a princess on her special day.(n)

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Jamie Laing and Sophie 'Habbs' Habboo revisit old editions of the programme, looking back to when the object of Jamie's desire was newcomer Lucy Watson.(n)

programme.title: Horton Hears a Who! (2008)
programme.desc: A kindly elephant hears a cry of help coming from a speck of dust and discovers the minuscule city of Whoville. As he tries to help its citizens, the other animals begin to question his sanity, and the evil Kangaroo incites them to turn against the do-gooder. Animated adaptation of Dr Seuss's book, with the voices of Jim Carrey, Steve Carell, Carol Burnett, Seth Rogen and Will Arnett.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host this week is Jay Hutton from Tattoo Fixers, whose guests are actor Steven Arnold and Ewen MacIntosh and reality stars Charlotte Crosby and Dawn Ward.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Steven Arnold hosts his first ever dinner party, and unwittingly makes the same chicken and mushroom dish that Jay Hutton did the previous night.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Real Housewives of Cheshire star Dawn Ward hosts the third dinner party of the week, and she pulls out all the stops to throw the perfect dinner party for her famous guests.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Actor Ewen MacIntosh of The Office fame serves up an evening of Mexican food, and leaves a lasting impression on his fellow celebrities - for all the wrong reasons.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charlotte Crosby takes the reins for the final night of the week's competition, with the Geordie Shore star aiming for a party finale with an après-ski-theme. The soirée kicks off with fondue and Charlotte impresses her guests with personalised poems, but after a disastrous DIY dessert she needs to save her night with some banging tunes and a live saxophone player.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam picks a team of popular athletes over his own friends in gym class, his mates seek revenge by forcing their opponents to play a game of Dungeons and Dragons. Murray objects when Erica decides that she wants to go away from home to attend college. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Barry insists that Beverly always favours Adam, she tries to make amends by smothering her oldest son with affection - much to his horror. Murray is not happy about Erica's new boyfriend, a fanatical music fan who is trying to convert her to his way of thinking. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry is shocked when an attractive teaching assistant agrees to go out with him, and Adam and Dana worry that they have missed their chance with their new crushes.(n)

programme.title: Schooled
programme.desc: Lainey figures out her feelings in time for Valentine's Day. Felicia is struggling with body image issues, so Coach Mellor throws a workout camp to help Felicia's self-esteem.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon helps Leonard drop off a parcel for Penny, but when he sees how messy her room is, he tries to do a good deed - which is not well received. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is upset to learn Penny is dating someone, so his friends encourage him to seek a new romance by asking another woman out. Sara Gilbert appears alongside her Roseanne co-star Johnny Galecki.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is fired for being disrespectful to his boss, and explores new activities in a bid to find a purpose in life other than physics, but Leonard takes drastic action to restore him to his old self. Laurie Metcalf guest stars as Sheldon's mother.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard fears he is wasting his time with Penny and ends up spending the night with fellow scientist Leslie. However, he is disappointed when she later tells him she was only interested in a one-night stand. Meanwhile, Sheldon frets over a change to his hamburger routine. Starring Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Jim Parsons.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny hosts a Halloween costume party, which proves a worthy test for the boys' social skills. During the event, Leonard has a run-in with Penny's ex-boyfriend. Kaley Cuoco, Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg star.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is forced to take Howard's place in the boys' video-game night while he is otherwise engaged with one of her promiscuous friends. Simon Helberg, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki star.(n)

programme.title: Paddington (2014)
programme.desc: An earthquake forces a bear from the Peruvian jungle to start a new life in London. He finds modern Britain to be very different from the stories told to him by his aunt, but makes a new home with a kindly family. However, a sinister taxidermist intends to turn him into a museum exhibit. Comedy based on Michael Bond's series of children's books, starring Hugh Bonneville, Sally Hawkins and Nicole Kidman, with the voice of Ben Whishaw.(n)

programme.title: Batwoman
programme.desc: Alice celebrates her ultimate act of vengeance, Mary focuses on Jacob Kane's trial, while Sophie seeks advice about her love life from an unexpected source.(n)

programme.title: Misfits
programme.desc: Curtis tries to go back in time to change the events of the night he was caught by the police, but his meddling brings about further complications. Sally discovers Tony's credit card in Simon's locker and becomes convinced the group is involved in his disappearance. Comedy drama, starring Lauren Socha, Nathan Stewart-Jarrett, Iwan Rheon, Alex Reid and Robert Sheehan.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ray, from Swindon, is fed up of dating bad boys and wants to find a nice guy, not a player. Australian Jimmy has been travelling the world for eight years and has decided to settle down in Bristol. However, one thing is missing - a perfect partner. Anna Richardson assists both singletons, inviting them to choose their favourite partner from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time until fully nude.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Glamorgan-based couple Richard and Felicity Arcelli want a second home in the Coimbra region of Portugal and are looking to spend £40,000 on a renovation project that builder Richard can turn into a permanent home. Can presenter Sara Damergi find them their dream property?(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip and Janet call on friends and family as they're down to the wire trying to finish the bridal suite in time for their daughter Penny's wedding. Mariam and Johnno get to work preparing all their bedrooms as they're due to host seven guests in three weeks' time. It's far from easy as Mariam struggles to find enough material to make curtains and finds out that a custom-made mattress doesn't fit an antique bed. Emma and Paul complete their Charles Rennie Mackintosh-themed bedroom in time for Emma's brother George's visit to celebrate his 60th birthday.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton returns to East Sussex to catch up with Steve and with an eco-efficient, straw-baled self-built home set in a woodland glade. After a long and testing process, Charlie's on-site as this incredible project finally reaches the finishing line.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Built nearly a century before the British canal system, the Canal du Midi in the south of France has been designated a world heritage site. Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore a picturesque stretch, as is it meanders toward the Mediterranean. They visit the town of Beziers and the Malpas Tunnel - Europe's first navigable canal tunnel - and taste the delights of lamb's brain fritters, as well as brandy that is almost 200 years old.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul prepare for their biggest chateau booking ever as they host American veterans for the 75th anniversary of D-Day. Mariam and Johnno transform their chateau's grand salon by installing reproduction Parquet de Versailles flooring and hand-painted murals. Plus, Karen and Paul aim to transform the old stable and coach house into a space fit for hosting weddings.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Mary Martin and Carl Turner want to build a home that resembles a giant stack of glass cubes in Brixton, south London. The couple are hoping the structure will become an instant landmark, while on the inside they are planning a Zen retreat. But to succeed, their finances must be pushed to their limit, so Carl takes on as many of the specialist jobs as he can to save money. Kevin McCloud follows their progress.(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: The death of a psychologist who assesses the suitability of soldiers for special combat missions causes a great stir. Valeria and the team are presented with more than one suspect, including the wife of a possible lover and a couple of soldiers involved in drug-running. The members of the homicide unit are tasked with sifting through conflicting stories and motives to get at the real killer while circumventing interference aimed at them from the higher echelons of the military. Crime drama, with Miriam Leone. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Newbury Park in southern California to help Mexican restaurant Mama Rita's. The owner previously ran a successful catering company but has struggled to adapt her skills to meet the demands of a new business - and with a year's worth of food stored in the freezer Gordon realises he has a tough job ahead.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits a green bean plantation in Kenya to find out how they grow the beans so straight for the supermarkets, and finds out what happens to those that don't make the grade. At a Norwegian scallop farm, Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join the divers harvesting scallops by hand from the seabed, and Kate Quilton tries to unravel a mystery about Cornwall's most famous local delicacy.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the paper crafts competition at the Royal Welsh Show. She produces an embroidered greetings card, and decoupage designer Jaina Minton teaches her how to make a paper sculpture of a seagull. To create jewellery, Phiona Richards lends a helping hand recycling damaged books and turning the print and pictures into rose rings.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Brenda and Nick Kennett from Surrey want to buy a family holiday home in the Correze region of France. Sara Damergi seeks contenders for their £60,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps to find the perfect holiday home to be shared by three sisters and their brother in the Poitou-Charentes region of France.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sama Damergi helps Paula Brown to pursue her dream of retiring to the Vendee region of western France. With a budget of £80,000, Paula hopes she will be able to buy a property in which she can keep chickens and grow vegetables.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps first-time buyers Lucy and Joe, who have set their heart on a cottage in the Cotswolds, where they can start a family. However, Joe wants a project and has already discounted hundreds of potential homes online, so it looks like he's going to be hard to please. Phil's search is just as difficult in Bristol, where country boy Paul and city girl Elly want a home with outside space that's close to the railway station. Finding somewhere that ticks all the boxes could be tricky.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is in the countryside surrounding Edinburgh helping Ian find a new home - but it soon becomes clear that he is more interested in finding the right shed for his collection of cars and tools than somewhere to lay his head. Phil Spencer is with Rogan and Jane, who suffer from chronic indecision when it comes to house hunting - and that's the least thing they need as they have to find a home fast before their first baby arrives in three months' time.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre-lover Claire Flatt hosts the first party in Ipswich, where one guest makes a big impression by arriving in a limousine. Claire prepares Thai fishcakes and Princess Jasmine rice, as well as her signature dish 'chaos cake', in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize at the end of the week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Millwall fan Kerrie Pankhurst hosts the second dinner party in Ipswich, which she decides to give a football theme - but the mood turns sour when one of the guests insults her team. Kerrie's decision to serve scallops and chorizo as her starter also does not go down well with DJ Dave James.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Magician David J Marks hosts the third dinner party in Ipswich, and hopes to win over Kerrie Pankhurst after offending her the previous night. His salmon starter impresses all the diners except Dave James, who explains all the gruesome things he would rather do than eat the fish, so the host lightens the mood by showing off his ventriloquist dummies and laying on a magic show.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Dave James hosts the fourth dinner party from Ipswich and asks his guests to attend dressed as pop stars. Claire Flatt and Nicki Yucel are suspicious about his celebrity connections, and there is trouble when the diners discover Dave obtained help with the cooking.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Speed-dating expert Nicki Yucel hosts the final dinner party in Ipswich, and is determined to cultivate a romantic atmosphere. As the evening progresses, Kerrie Pankhurst reveals her feelings for another diner, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow B&B owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival B&B owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next day's breakfast fails to impress.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Lysette and Nigel Offley, who are knocking down an old Thames boathouse and constructing a cutting-edge home in its place. Taking inspiration from the river itself, they have designed a building of waves and curves with a huge glass porthole for a roof light. But problems begin almost from the start, as they cannot find a contractor willing to build over the water, their architect leaves unexpectedly and the costs turn out much higher than expected. To make matters worse, the neighbours are not happy about such a radical sight in their traditional community. As they begin to compromise, Kevin wonders if the couple's dreams will ever be realised.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow couples as they cope with treatment, including Corey, who's 27. He is rushed to St George's after suffering a cardiac arrest during a game of ice hockey, and his wife of seven days, Alice, was watching him play when he collapsed. There's also a profile of John, 55. He fell 10 feet while taking down scaffolding at home, and his spouse Penny talks about how he has supported her through sickness and bereavement during their marriage.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Clare Grogan guest stars as a feminist protest singer who visits Craggy Island and talks gormless Dougal into giving her the parochial house while Ted is out judging a local beauty contest. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priest makes a valiant effort to give up cigarettes for Lent, inspired by a challenge from Dick Byrne, his opposite number over on Rugged Island, but soon finds he is not cut out for a life of self-denial. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan teaming up against Noel Fielding and Fay Ripley. The Brett Domino Trio are in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Le Bistro in Lighthouse Point, Florida, where the stubborn chef and owner has lost his passion for cooking. The visiting expert hopes to help, but his criticisms are not well received, causing tempers to flare.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Clare Grogan guest stars as a feminist protest singer who visits Craggy Island and talks gormless Dougal into giving her the parochial house while Ted is out judging a local beauty contest. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and her team help Shaun and Jannine Cartwright and their three sons transform their Blackburn home, which is full of clutter. They look to the 1950s for inspiration, creating a family playroom for the boys.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Duncan, who has always loved Fiats, bought his iconic 1967 Fiat 500 with the aim of restoring it in memory of his late wife. However, after a second family tragedy when his youngest son died, grieving Duncan struggled to find his motivation and the car sat rusting in a damp garage. While Fuzz Townshend puzzles over the Fiat's tiny engine, Tim Shaw goes into shock when the car's shell comes back from the acid dippers. The boys go on a road trip to Turin and, as always, it's a race against time to get the Italian job done.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: The Sunbeam Alpine, a sleek two-seater convertible, was one of the most stylish British sports cars of the 1960s, and is now back in fashion and much sought after. Former mechanic and motor racing enthusiast Ray bought his Sunbeam Alpine over 20 years ago as a retirement project. Ray was always an active cyclist and skier, but after he suffered a brain injury six years ago he was left with severe disabilities. Knowing how much the car means to him, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend use all their motoring know-how and parts-blagging guile to get Ray's car back on the road.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Caterham 7 belonging to a former amateur racer, who had to focus on caring for his family after his wife was diagnosed with cancer.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in south-west London to help another two couples find their dream home. Long-distance partners Jason and Jaye are looking for a one-bed pad with outside space in Clapham or Brixton to finally bring them together, while Hannah and Frank want a family home before they get married - but have viewed nearly 50 properties without finding the one they want.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Cardiff to help another two couples find their dream home. Jo and Steve are relocating from Bristol to cut down on her commute to work, and Kirstie is on hand to provide them with advice on suitable properties for their needs. Phil accompanies Shauna and Todd, who admit their pricey taste in property is making it tough to find a family home.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Horton's passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carpenter Wes Leaver hosts the first party in east Kent, hoping his science theme will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize at the end of the week. However, the 34-year-old faces competition from estate agent Alexandra Austin, beautician Emma Phillips, dog-lover Sally Parry and fancy-dress enthusiast Stephen Drew.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Alexandra Austen hosts the second party of the week in east Kent, hoping to impress her guests with pan-European cuisine. However, the meal gets off to a shaky start when Sally Parry is forced to confront her fear of beans, while Stephen Drew struggles with a goat's cheese parcel. Alexandra's decision to serve veal also proves controversial, and it soon becomes clear two of the guests are not enjoying each other's company.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beautician Emma Phillips hosts the third dinner party in east Kent. Her main course is described by one diner as a pet on a plate, and Alexandra Austin declares the menu to be rather odd. Meanwhile, Stephen Drew and Wes Leaver enjoy an impromptu beauty treatment together.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-lover Sally Parry hosts the fourth dinner party in east Kent, hoping to impress her guests with a pink-themed night, including posh pink food and table decorations. However, Sally and Emma have a heated argument, Alex misbehaves and Wes falls asleep.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant Stephen Drew hosts the final dinner party in east Kent, and has designed a menu that will allow him to spend time entertaining his guests. With an Absolutely Fabulous theme, a few of the diners are expecting a Mardi Gras vibe, but he has different ideas for the night - before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Leigh Osborne and Graham Voce want to convert and extend a landmark 150-year-old water tower in Kennington, south London, into a luxury home. Grade II-listed, derelict and with 6ft-thick walls, it's a huge challenge for the pair, who also plan to build two structures at the base - a lift shaft connected by a series of glass tunnels, and a modern living space. The result will be a four-bedroom property over nine floors, complete with a room at the top offering spectacular 360-degree views across the capital. Presented by Kevin McCloud.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

Other officers pursue suspected joyriders, and are shocked to find the driver is only 14.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter continues his examination of how the car became an everyday part of people's lives, exploring the weird world of small vehicles. He discovers how austerity and fears of congestion led to European disasters in the shape of Britain's infamous three-wheelers, French death traps and German absurdities, and also takes to the battlefield to settle one of the greatest rivalries in car history.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James concludes his examination of the social significance of cars by exploring how aspiration and new wealth were behind the development of some of the greatest models ever made. He tries to make sense of the baffling world of company car hierarchy by holding a travelling salesman race-off and indulges his 1980s urges with the twin delights of a Lamborghini and a Porsche. He also unveils his choice of the ultimate people's car - a vehicle that can lay claim to being the greatest in history.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear: Ambitious but Rubbish
programme.desc: Relive the Top Gear team's attempts to constructing a V8-powered rocking chair, building Top Gear's own electric car, and making the world's first caravan-based train.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee wants to create the perfect Christmas for Lucy, so he invites her to spend the holiday in a cosy old country house that used to belong to his dear, dead old aunt - and to make it a proper family get-together, he invites along her parents and dipsy friend Daisy. But the Christmas spirit is in short supply when Lucy discovers that the cottage has not been lived in for years - and it isn't long before they have sensed a spooky presence. Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix star in this festive special of the sitcom, with Bobby Ball, Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant. Festive edition from 2013.(n)

programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are challenged to build a bridge over the River Kwai in Thailand. In order to do that, however, they have to drive across Burma, a country that has been largely closed to Westerners for more than 40 years, and must make the trip in three lorries, each bought sight unseen from the internet on a limited budget. What follows is an epic journey of beautiful scenery, regular adversity, ongoing malfunction and the constant bickering of the three men.(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve Backshall leads a team into Yucatan, Mexico on the trail of the ancient Maya. Uncovering sinister secrets and artefacts, they push far into the underworld of the Maya.(n)

As usual, points are awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting, regardless of whether or not they are correct.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: All Girls Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A female-only special of the comedy show, showcasing some of the funniest women to have graced the stage at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The programme features Jo Brand, Joan Rivers, Aisling Bea, Kerry Godliman, Nina Conti, Luisa Omielan, Gina Yashere, Angela Barnes, Zoe Lyons, Holly Walsh, Desiree Burch and Tanyalee Davis.(n)

programme.title: The First Silent Night
programme.desc: Simon Callow discovers the origin of the Christmas carol and the story of the two Austrians who wrote it.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey profiles two of Britain's greatest wartime leaders. He begins by revealing how Winston Churchill came to write a biography of his ancestor John, who led the nation against Louis XIV of France. Starkey also explores how preparations for the Second World War were affected by the prime minister's knowledge of his 17th-century forebear's achievements.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates conflicting accounts of the killing of Italian dictator Benito Mussolini in 1945.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: A look at how British-trained Czechs were behind the assassination of Reinhard Heydrich, one of the main architects of the Holocaust.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Broughton Castle, near Banbury, Oxfordshire. Among the finds brought in to be valued are an art nouveau vase, a Georgian dolls' house, a Victorian baby bouncer, a letter from Lord Nelson, a book of early police mug shots, a collection of talismans and a musical penknife. Plus, Philip Mould meets a tax inspector who was paid in paintings by several artists.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: How some of our greatest railways have challenged engineering's finest minds in their efforts to conquer the planet's waterways, including a tunnel beneath the English Channel.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A little-known dark past explains the vast monolithic structures that dominate the small British island of Guernsey.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Durham Cathedral, where she examines a collection of rare books, including an early version of the Magna Carta, and John Axford examines a rare 19th-century plate. Objects brought in to be valued include a carriage clock found on a bomb site, an early drawing by Laura Knight, a 19th-century missionary's medical kit and a collection of football memorabilia.(n)

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: The final leg of the river's journey sees it join its tributary the Brahmaputra. As the waters merge, they overspill their banks to fan out into hundreds of smaller rivulets and create the world's largest delta. The programme reveals how people and wildlife survive the inhospitable habitat, perhaps the last place on Earth where the Bengal tiger is still king.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the world's carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: A look at the planet's most incredible metropolitan railways, and how they help people solve distinctly urban problems. Including a project to upgrade New York's transit system.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How Second World War criminal Adolf Eichmann was tracked down to Buenos Aires, Argentina, in 1961.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Projects include a maze of tunnels filled with haunting remains beneath the streets of Paris and a strange formation of domed structures hovering over the waters off Florida.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Solar System
programme.desc: A team of scientists launch a probe to touch the sun and make a surprise discovery on the tiny planet Mercury.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates decide on a wager as the parole board gathers, with Blanco odds-on favourite for release. However, the old lag turns down his freedom and leaves Fletcher in danger of losing a small fortune. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber starts work on another O-level course, but wily old cellmate Fletcher can't understand why he doesn't just cheat in the exam. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch tries to ensure that Godber has not blown his chances of early release after he gets into a fight a week before his parole board hearing. Final episode of the classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length.(n)

He begins by revealing how Winston Churchill came to write a biography of his ancestor John, who led the nation against Louis XIV of France. Starkey also explores how preparations for the Second World War were affected by the prime minister's knowledge of his 17th-century forebear's achievements.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre uses the SAS archives to examine the history of the British Army special forces unit combining documents, unseen footage and interviews with its founding members to tell the remarkable story behind an extraordinary fighting force. In the first edition, he looks at its formation in the heat of the north African desert in 1941, when eccentric young British army officer David Archibald Stirling had a vision for a new kind of war in which the enemy was attacked from behind their own lines.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The varied landscape of the tropical islands and the species that inhabit them, from flamingos and four-eyed fish to anteaters and cave-dwelling rodents. The programme also delves deeper to explore the islands' destructive history of volcanic eruptions, dangerous reefs and powerful hurricanes.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The coral and shipwrecks found off the shores of the tropical islands, and how the different kinds of reef have given rise to a huge diversity of underwater life in the area. Underwater time-lapse photography also shows how carnivorous corals feed at night and how, over a few nights of the year, they disperse their sperm and eggs.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The effects of the region's powerful hurricanes on the islands' wildlife and the survival stories of some of the species, including how the Puerto Rican parrot almost became extinct. Offshore, lobsters head to deeper waters for refuge, while reefs benefit from the destruction.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The region's mysterious side comes to light during a journey from the rainforests of Panama to the Barrier Reef of the Americas - the world's second largest coral system. The programme also explores the wildlife on the shores of Central America, including killer whales and thimble jellyfish in the deep waters near Honduras.(n)

programme.title: Wild France
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring thousands of years of history, landscape and nature in France, as well as thriving wildlife from the Alps to the Pyrenees, as well as venturing from the mainland to Corsica.(n)

programme.title: Wild France
programme.desc: Part two of two. The unique ecosystem of the Marais Poitevin, where seawater and freshwater come together. Dragonfly larvae gestate in the region's waters, and swim to the surface and fly away once their development is over.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The varied landscape of the tropical islands and the species that inhabit them, from flamingos and four-eyed fish to anteaters and cave-dwelling rodents. The programme also delves deeper to explore the islands' destructive history of volcanic eruptions, dangerous reefs and powerful hurricanes.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The coral and shipwrecks found off the shores of the tropical islands, and how the different kinds of reef have given rise to a huge diversity of underwater life in the area. programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The effects of the region's powerful hurricanes on the islands' wildlife and the survival stories of some of the species, including how the Puerto Rican parrot almost became extinct. Offshore, lobsters head to deeper waters for refuge, while reefs benefit from the destruction.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The region's mysterious side comes to light during a journey from the rainforests of Panama to the Barrier Reef of the Americas - the world's second largest coral system. The programme also explores the wildlife on the shores of Central America, including killer whales and thimble jellyfish in the deep waters near Honduras.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: The series examining some of the UK's landmarks continues, with a look at the history of subterranean London, uncovering Roman remains, plague pits and secret bunkers under the capital's streets.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre uses the SAS archives to examine the history of the British Army special forces unit combining documents, unseen footage and interviews with its founding members to tell the remarkable story behind an extraordinary fighting force. In the first edition, he looks at its formation in the heat of the north African desert in 1941, when eccentric young British army officer David Archibald Stirling had a vision for a new kind of war in which the enemy was attacked from behind their own lines.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The varied landscape of the tropical islands and the species that inhabit them, from flamingos and four-eyed fish to anteaters and cave-dwelling rodents. The programme also delves deeper to explore the islands' destructive history of volcanic eruptions, dangerous reefs and powerful hurricanes.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water.(n)

programme.title: Wild North
programme.desc: Documentary examining three landscapes in northern Scandinavia. In the mountainous highlands, cameras capture two endangered predators relying on a plentiful supply of lemmings.(n)

programme.title: Wild North
programme.desc: Deep in the forest, a female bear and three cubs emerge in the sunshine after hibernation. The first priority for the mother is to find food - and quickly, or her cubs will starve.(n)

programme.title: Wild North
programme.desc: Far north in Svalbard, the polar bear depends on the ice to hunt seals. But with the ice receding, it has been forced to turn its attentions to a larger, trickier prey - the walrus.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the connections between the wildlife, landscape and people of the Arabian peninsula. The first edition features animals including oryxes, jerboas, horned vipers and scorpions, and a journey with the Bedouin - camel-riding nomads who are the only humans to have mastered the art of survival in the desert. Narrated by Alexander Siddig.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Exploring the mountains of Dhofar in Oman, where the annual Indian Ocean monsoon has helped create a lost world of waterfalls and cloud forests filled with fascinating wildlife. The programme shows green sea turtles coming ashore in their thousands, shadowed by egg-stealing foxes, as well as footage of striped hyenas doing battle with Arabian wolves. Plus, a glimpse into the lives of chameleons, honey badgers and the elusive Arabian leopard.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: The changes that have swept across the Arabian peninsula since the discovery of oil. Camel racing has become a hi-tech sport and the region's animals now live alongside a very modern society, but technology is also being used to protect nature. The programme reveals how dugongs are fitted with satellite transmitters and hunting falcons chase down radio-controlled planes, and follows the construction of the world's first carbon-neutral city. Narrated by Alexander Siddig.(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Documentary examining the famous Second World War evacuation of Allied soldiers - codenamed Operation Dynamo - from the beaches of Dunkirk, France, between May 26 and June 4, 1940. Recently released Ministry of Defence files reveal that, far from being absent as many historians have believed, the RAF was suffering massive losses supporting the evacuation. Operating at high altitude and deep inside France, RAF planes were halting the bulk of Luftwaffe bombers before they could reach the beaches. The files also reveal that the famous Spitfire took flight for the first time during the mission, and the agile fighter was a spectacular success.(n)

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club

programme.sub-title: If They Do Go Either Way, They're Usually Fake

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake gets close to one of Charlie's girlfriends, but when his uncle declares he will not be seeing her again, the youngster decides to make him suffer. programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is devastated to learn Judith has found a new boyfriend, and begins to wallow in gloom. To help boost his confidence, Charlie and Jake try to give him a makeover, but eventually admit defeat and bring in a gay man to choose the perfect outfit.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie meets Berta's grand-daughter Prudence, who quickly takes an unwanted interest in him. He asks Rose to pretend she is still his girlfriend as a cover - but the lovelorn old flame takes her role a little too seriously. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: A long-time crush of Charlie finally agrees to go on a date with him, as long as she can bring her sister - who will also need an escort. Alan is persuaded to step in, even though he has caught flu from Jake, but the entire evening is soon jeopardised. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A man has to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents. Music entrepreneur Donovan has not received rent from one of his tenants for seven months, so the case ends up in court.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with tenants and landlords from previous episodes, including a man living in a damp and mouldy flat, who was unable to sleep in the bedroom due to the horrendous conditions. The programme also revisits a 70-year-old landlady whose tenants stopped paying the rent for a year, and the council's Rogue Landlord Team in Lewisham, who learned where a slum property owner's reputation for a short fuse came from.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A woman owes five months' rent, but refuses to leave unless the bailiffs arrive, while a man wanted to sell his house post-breakup, but the tenants are still there five years later. Plus, a man happily living in his garden shed.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord seeks urgent advice from eviction specialist Chris after finding his three-bedroom terrace has been stripped bare of the fittings, the flooring, and even the grass on the lawn. Landlady Rose gets a shock when she finally gains access to her property, and Dave from Peterborough Council's housing team comes across some of the worst damp and mould he has ever seen. Plus, Selina and Lee must return from America for a court case to recover £10,000 rent arrears on their UK property.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Kate faces trouble from unruly tenants, who became anti-social, and are now trying to sue her for serving them an eviction notice. Dhiren relates how the existing tenant of a house he bought stopped paying rent as soon as he took ownership, and Tim explains how a health scare left him seeking to sell his property - but a standing occupant refused to leave.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement officers visit a former professional footballer who owes £4,500 in unpaid legal fees, and in London the officers chase up a £4,000 debt owed by a car dealer. Elsewhere, agents Gary Brown and Cona Jackson are involved in a serious altercation with a young man who owes almost £11,000, who threatens the officers with a knife when they start listing goods to seize to offset the debt.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers chase a debt of almost £2,000 in unpaid parking fines, and later encounter the extraordinary case of a debt of £80,000 owed by a hotel owner to his ex-girlfriend. However, as they begin to search the property, they call upon police back-up as the agents fear the debtor may have access to firearms. Elsewhere, two officers head to a car garage in Manchester where the owner owes £2,000 after losing a court case over a faulty vehicle.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents chase more than £1,700 in unpaid parking fines in south-east London, but struggle to pinpoint the debtor. Meanwhile, officers in north Wales try to recover the £2,000 owed by a tradesman to a former employer, and in Kent agents chase £8,000 owed by a jewellery shop to two former employees.(n)

programme.title: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves (1991)
programme.desc: English nobleman Robin of Locksley returns home from fighting in the Crusades, only to discover that his father has been murdered and his lands seized, while the local people are living in fear under the tyrannical rule of the Sheriff of Nottingham. Branded an outlaw for his defiance, he and his Moorish travelling companion go into hiding in the forest, where they recruit a group of bandits to fight for justice, protect the downtrodden, and foil the Sheriff's plan to seize the throne while the king is away at war. Medieval action adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Alan Rickman, Morgan Freeman, Mary Elizabeth Mastrantonio, Christian Slater and Mike McShane.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

Military crime drama, with Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo and David McCallum.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Bull

programme.desc: Cabot Cove's new priest takes a murderer's confession, and asks Jessica to help him uncover the truth without breaking his sacred oath of absolute confidentiality. Starring Angela Lansbury.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The death of an elderly writer prompts Jessica to recall an intriguing mystery that happened aboard the Queen Mary some 50 years previously. Guest starring Robert Vaughn, with Angela Lansbury and June Havoc.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher recounts the unusual tale of a private eye who reluctantly recruited a new partner in the form of a French poodle, which once belonged to a murder victim. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A volunteer firefighter is found dead in a burnt-out furniture store. However, Jessica's suspicions are aroused when the post-mortem suggests he was deceased before the blaze started. Angela Lansbury stars.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits the opera and finds that, amid the hysteria and emotion of the singing world, a murder has taken place that appears at first to be a case of self-defence. A world-famous tenor faces a battle to clear his name. Angela Lansbury and Keith Michell star.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica finds herself in the middle of a wealthy family squabbling over the inheritance of a multi-millionaire, and spends the weekend at his isolated island retreat. programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica meets an old friend who happens to be one of the best homicide detectives in Los Angeles, and helps provide the vital clue in the case that ended his career. Starring Angela Lansbury and Barry Newman.(n)

programme.desc: The police discover a 12-year-old boy posted sinister threats to a girl in an internet chatroom and suspect his anger stems from sexual abuse. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An assistant district attorney is found strangled in his car in an area notorious for prostitution. Benson is shocked to realise she briefly dated the victim and refuses to believe he would have paid for sex. As investigations get under way, DNA evidence reveals the dead man might have been gay. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler tries to rescue a youngster who has been kidnapped by a man with schizophrenia, but resorts to unorthodox tactics that jeopardise his own career. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man is arrested for assaulting his alcoholic wife, who is pregnant - but changes the focus of the case by trying to have her charged with neglect for drinking while expecting a baby. programme.title: Bull

programme.title: Columbo: The Greenhouse Jungle (1972)
programme.desc: A penniless rare-plant collector, desperate for funds to support his expensive hobby, fakes his nephew's kidnapping to get his hands on a trust fund. However, the supposed victim turns up dead, prompting Columbo to take action to unmask the killer. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Bradford Dillman and Ray Milland.(n)

programme.title: Columbo Goes to College (1991)
programme.desc: Two criminology students murder their professor and cover their tracks with false clues. They think they are on the way to outwitting everyone - but find themselves at the bottom of the class when Columbo intervenes. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Stephen Caffrey, Gary Hershberger and Robert Culp.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Dangerous Match (1973)
programme.desc: An American chess champion fears he is about to be beaten by a superior player from Russia, and decides the only sure-fire way he can avoid defeat is by murdering his opponent. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Laurence Harvey and Jack Kruschen.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Uneasy Lies the Crown (1990)
programme.desc: A dentist with gambling debts discovers his wife is having an affair with one of his patients and wants a divorce. He plans to take revenge by poisoning the lover with his wife's heart medication, so she will be suspected - but he reckons without the attentions of the crumpled cop. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, James Read, Paul Burke, Jo Anderson and Nancy Walker.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Any Old Port in a Storm (1973)
programme.desc: A vineyard owner's financial security is jeopardised when his playboy younger brother threatens to sell the family winery. He resorts to killing his sibling and rigging the death scene to look like a scuba-diving accident. However, the crumpled cop sees through the ruse and in the process reveals he is something of a wine buff himself. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Donald Pleasence and Julie Harris.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Ashes to Ashes (1998)
programme.desc: The shabby sleuth investigates the complex case of a murdered Hollywood gossip writer. He uses his unique talents to interrogate a smooth-talking funeral director, who was once romantically involved with the victim and one of the last people to see her alive. Detective drama, with Peter Falk. Patrick McGoohan co-stars and directs.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A Ukrainian illegal immigrant working as a prostitute is murdered in a motel room, setting in motion an investigation that leads to a writer secretly profiling the singles scene. At first his guilt seems a foregone conclusion - but as inquiries continue, Falacci and Logan begin to suspect he is being framed. Crime drama, starring Chris Noth and Alicia Witt.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The rape and murder of a criminology student puts detectives Stabler and Benson on the trail of a killer with an expert knowledge of forensics. A discovery about the victim's mentor places him at the top of their list of suspects - but as further evidence is gathered, it begins to seem that someone might have tried to frame him for the crime.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Detective Munch becomes personally involved in the prosecution of a terminally ill embryologist who is accused of assisting a depressed woman in committing suicide. Evidence points overwhelmingly to her guilt - but the jury finds it hard to decide her fate. Hard-hitting police drama, guest starring Marlee Matlin.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: First episode of the fantasy drama charting the legendary wizard's formative years, starring Colin Morgan and Anthony Head. Merlin is sent by his mother to start a new life in Camelot, where he witnesses the execution of a man for using magic and learns he must tread carefully from now on. When he later steps in to protect a manservant from Prince Arthur and encounters the Great Dragon, the young sorcerer begins an adventure that will determine his destiny.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Knight Valiant arrives in Camelot to compete in the annual sword-fighting tournament and faces Arthur in the final. Merlin realises the combatant has a ruse to beat his opponents that could spell death for the prince - but struggles to convince King Uther of his findings. Will Mellor guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Bayard and the knights of the Western Isles arrive in Camelot for a feast celebrating a union between the two kingdoms. Nimueh poses as a serving girl and infiltrates the festivities with a view to gaining revenge on Merlin, poisoning the warlock and sending him into a coma. Clive Russell guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Lancelot arrives in Camelot and leaps to Merlin's rescue when he is attacked by a griffin. The warlock learns of the aspiring knight's lack of nobility and fabricates credentials to help his new friend achieve his ambition. However, the ruse is uncovered and Lancelot is thrown in jail. Former Heroes star Santiago Cabrera guests.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.sub-title: A Remedy to Cure All Ills
programme.desc: Morgana succumbs to a mysterious illness and a new physician arrives in Camelot just in time to cure her. Uther is impressed by the stranger's abilities but Gaius becomes suspicious and enlists the help of his friend Geoffrey of Monmouth to find out more about the newcomer. Guest starring Green Wing's Julian Rhind-Tutt and former Grange Hill actor Michael Cronin.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Arthur and Merlin are hunting in the forest when they rescue a man and his beautiful daughter from bandits. The prince is besotted with the girl and offers the pair refuge in Camelot - unaware the object of his affections poses a threat to his life. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: A druid and his boy apprentice are discovered in Camelot and try to avoid capture. The elder is caught and executed, but Merlin hears the juvenile's telepathic cries for help. Despite the Great Dragon's warnings to be wary, Merlin risks his life to protect the youngster, putting Arthur in danger.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Arthur's uncle Tristan De Bois returns from the grave in the form of a mysterious knight in black. Although intent on revenge for his sister's death, he could also reveal a secret from Uther's past. Gaius realises the only way he can be killed is with a sword forged in dragon's breath. Featuring the voice of Christopher Fairbank.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Bandits attack Merlin's home village, where his mother Hunith still lives - but with Uther unwilling to provoke a war by crossing the border to repel them, Arthur joins forces with Morgana and Gwen. However, an old friend of the wizard's, William, accuses Arthur of provoking the situation. With Alexander Siddig and Joe Dempsie.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: After Arthur kills a unicorn while hunting, Camelot's crops are destroyed and its water supply runs dry. The prince refuses to believe he could be responsible for any curse, but is warned by the animal's keeper that he must make amends or the kingdom will face ruin. Frank Finlay guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Gwen's father Tom is put on trial by Uther, accused of practising magic by helping a sorcerer turn lead into gold. However, Morgana fears his fate has already been decided, and tries to help him escape before he is sentenced to death. Cal Macaninch guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Arthur is mortally wounded by a monster that has the body of a lion and the head of a serpent. Merlin seeks out followers of an ancient religion who have the power to reverse death, but he faces a tough decision after learning that another life must be sacrificed for the prince to survive.(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A burglar is spotted climbing into a bathroom window by a neighbour, and the owner of a hairdressing salon rushes to his salon to try and catch thieves in the act.(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: More true crimes, including a house burglar who met his match at the foot of the stairs and a man broke into a garage and ended up having an early bath - in engine oil.(n)

programme.title: Micky Flanagan's Detour de France
programme.desc: The comedian hops across the Channel and jumps on a bicycle to sample the sights and sounds of France, joined by his best friend Noel Lynch, a brickie and cycling enthusiast. The pair start off in Calais with some essential pampering, fully aware that no cyclist worth his or her salt would hit the road without a facial or a leg wax. Then it's bon voyage as they tackle a small portion of the 150-mile race between Paris and Roubaix lovingly known as the Hell of the North. The cobbles may look pretty - but they aren't bike-friendly.(n)

programme.title: Micky Flanagan's Detour de France
programme.desc: The comedian and his friend Noel Lynch stop off to sample a few glasses of bubbly at some of the region's premier champagne houses. Later, a spot of nudity is called for when Micky picks the brains of a British couple who like to frequent a naturist camp, while Noel takes on one of cycling's greatest challenges.(n)

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: The documentary returns to focus on policing in the north-east of England. Officers race to a stabbing outside a pub in Newcastle and there is a raid on a cannabis farm.(n)

programme.title: The Force: Manchester
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Greater Manchester Police. The force are thrown into action as the emergency calls rush in.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Documentary examining the kidnapping of journalists Scott Taylor and Zeynep Tugrul by insurgents in Iraq in 2004. They were subjected to threats of torture and execution after being abducted from the city of Tal Afar, and passed around a number of militant groups before being freed.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of Australian Stephen Sutton, who believed a $15,000 deal to smuggle cocaine from Argentina to his country would pay off.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of a man who was imprisoned for seven years after dealers threatened to attack his family if he did not smuggle cocaine out of Peru.(n)

programme.desc: Renowned dog trainer Cesar Millan steps in to stop two troublesome Alsatians from their persistent fighting.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Orang-utan Aris has his knee injury checked by the vet and it turns out to be worse than expected, so he goes for an operation. Chimp Patricia finally begins to relax in the company of alpha male Hananya.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Military aircraft are dispatched to transport 88 capuchin monkeys from their Chilean home to their new residence at Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The Chilean capuchins arrive in the UK, and there is bad news in the orang-utan house. Meanwhile, the keepers worry that something has gone seriously wrong for Aris since his knee operation.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The alpha male chimps assert their authority amidst signs of a brewing coup. The baby woolly monkey is finally given a name, and Aris is still under the weather.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Eighty-eight capuchin monkeys rescued from a research laboratory in Chile wake up to their first morning at the Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset. Young chimp Rodders has trouble with a sticky melon, and the park's naughtiest resident, Seamus, comes face to face with alpha male Butch.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Wildlife vet John Lewis is given a run for his money as he tries to sedate two excitable chimps. The recently rescued male capuchins get used to life together, and orang-utan Kai proves too energetic for her companions.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The team is forced to step in when Ben is injured by the alpha male during the chimp introduction. Orang-utan Hsiao-Quai struggles with her ever-growing son Kai, and staff at Monkey World prepare to welcome a new arrival.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A four-month-old woolly monkey is hand-reared, and there is excitement in the endangered golden-cheeked gibbons' enclosure when it is revealed Zoey may be pregnant.(n)

programme.title: Blood & Treasure
programme.sub-title: The Curse of Cleopatra - Part One
programme.desc: When his mentor is abducted after discovering the tomb of Mark Antony and Cleopatra, art expert Danny McNamara recruits the help of an old flame Action adventure, starring Matt Barrand Sofia Pernas.(n)

programme.title: The Good Doctor
programme.desc: Shaun has to confront prejudice from an unlikely source when he takes on the case of a patient with autism. Medical drama, starring Freddie Highmore.(n)

programme.title: Queen - Live in Rio
programme.desc: The band's historic opening performance at the Rock in Rio festival in 1985, playing Love of My Life, Tie Your Mother Down and Radio Gaga to an audience of more than 250,000.(n)

programme.title: Freddie Mercury: The Great Pretender
programme.desc: Profile of the Queen frontman, exploring his time away from the band - including a collaboration with Michael Jackson and the triumphant Barcelona duet with Montserrat Caballe. The programme also examines his life as a gay man who was not yet publicly out. Featuring archive footage and interviews with bandmates Brian May and Roger Taylor, as well as friends and admirers including Paul Gambaccini, Montserrat Caballe and Matt Lucas.(n)

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: The case of Harold Jones, found guilty of murder at the age of 15 in 1921, who is also suspected to have been 1960s serial killer 'Jack the Stripper'.(n)
